rationale: Major frictions: live-action UGC dance reactions clash hard with TheSphinx's "Mystical Minimalism" dark/gold premium aesthetic — the blue-lit teen energy reads casual/Gen-Z hypercasual, not the sophisticated 25-45 trivia audience. The hook also fails to communicate the core loop (deductive Q&A with an AI to guess a celebrity), which is a concept-driven mechanic that needs to be shown, not emoted around. Only worth testing as a low-budget hook variant if reframed around an "I can't believe the AI guessed [celebrity]" reaction with on-screen gameplay overlay; otherwise skip.
